Singing from a distant rock spire, mouth open to the storm, hair whipping wild, barely visible through salt spray and mist.
Tail vanishing beneath black waves, leaving a glowing phosphorescent trail spiraling down into impossible depths behind her.
Silhouetted on a sea stack against the full moon, arms raised skyward, hair cascading like dark water down her back.
Half-submerged in a tidal pool, watching a passing merchant ship with luminous unblinking eyes that never blink or look away.
Draped across a coral reef at low tide, combing endless wet hair with a pearlescent shell, humming something wordless.
Diving from a cliff into deep water, iridescent tail catching sunset's last light before vanishing into the depths below.
Floating on her back in calm moonlit water, tail fin breaking the surface lazily, sending ripples toward the horizon.
Perched on a ghost ship's figurehead, gazing out to sea, pale tail curled possessively around the rotting wooden prow.
Rising from a wave crest, seawater cascading off bare shoulders, gone completely before the next swell rolls through.
Visible beneath the surface as a glowing shape, seen from the ship's rail, keeping pace with the vessel's speed.
Sitting on a barnacle-covered rock, examining a drowned sailor's brass compass by moonlight, turning it curiously in pale fingers.
Slipping off a rock into dark water as the lighthouse beam sweeps past, missed by seconds, leaving only ripples.
Beckoning from shallow surf with one pale arm, half her face above water, the rest submerged in foam and shadow.
Braiding her hair on a sandbar at dawn, surrounded by mist, tail coiled beneath her, disappearing as sunlight breaks through.
Circling a rowboat in deep water, just her head visible, swimming closer with each slow, deliberate circuit around the hull.
Dragging something heavy beneath the surface near the reef, only her struggling shoulders visible above the rolling waves.
Singing to the stars from a half-submerged wreck, head tilted back, voice carrying impossibly far across still water.
Watching from behind a rock formation, only her eyes and wet hair visible, tracking a dinghy's progress toward shore.
Leaping through a moonbeam on the water, entire body arcing silver through the air before splashing down beyond sight.
Resting in floating kelp, arms tangled in the strands, tail drifting beneath, pale skin almost glowing against the seaweed.
Pulling herself onto a sun-warmed rock, water streaming from her hair and scales, stretching languidly like a cat.
Diving deep with something golden clutched to her chest, tail propelling her down until darkness swallows her completely.
Silhouetted against distant lightning, sitting upright on a rock, utterly still despite the rising storm waves crashing around her.
Singing a sailor's shanty back to a ship, her voice mocking and sweet, barely visible in the pre-dawn fog.
Swimming alongside dolphins at sunset, breaking surface together, her laughter carrying across the water like wind chimes.
Holding a mirror shard from a shipwreck, studying her reflection in moonlight, tail swaying gently in the current.
Reaching up from beneath a dock, pale arms stretching toward dangling feet above, fingers almost touching before withdrawing.
Lying across two rocks like a bridge, hair trailing in the water below, watching clouds drift overhead with ancient eyes.
Tangled in a fishing net, struggling briefly before slicing through it with something sharp, vanishing with the severed strands.
Rising vertically from calm water, arms at her sides, ascending impossibly high before falling backward with barely a splash.
Collecting shells from a tide pool, arranging them on a rock, tail fins flicking droplets that catch rainbow light.
Visible through a wave's translucent face, suspended inside the curl, arms outstretched, gone when the wave breaks white.
Sitting beneath an arch of stone at high tide, framed perfectly by the opening, combing her fingers through wet tangles.
Following a sea turtle through clear shallows, her shadow passing over sand below, tail undulating in perfect rhythm.
Emerging from between two rocks at dusk, seaweed draped across her shoulders like a shawl, eyes reflecting the dying light.
Clinging to a ship's anchor chain below the surface, looking up at the hull, hair drifting upward in the current.
Sunbathing on a remote skerry, tail hanging in the water, so still she might be carved from pale stone.
Dancing in the shallows during a storm, spinning with the waves, arms raised, laughing at the thunder and lightning.
Holding a drowned lantern, trying to light it with matches from a waterproof tin, frustrated and eerily human in the attempt.
Swimming backward away from shore, maintaining eye contact with someone on the beach, smiling with too many teeth.
Perched where river meets ocean, half in fresh water and half in salt, as if she exists between two worlds.
Floating vertically in deep water, only her head above surface, hair fanning out below like dark roots seeking downward.
Sitting in the curve of a massive wave before it breaks, riding it like a throne, serene and terrifying.
Stealing bait from a fishing line, quick silver movements beneath the boat, leaving the hook bare and the fisherman confused.
Visible in the bioluminescent wake of a ship, keeping pace effortlessly, her outline traced in blue-green ghostly fire.
Mourning on a rock over scattered bones, keening a song so sad even the wind seems to pause and listen.
Splashing playfully in the surf like a child, diving through breakers, then suddenly still and watching with predatory focus.
Examining a message in a bottle, holding it up to moonlight, trying to read the paper inside without breaking the glass.
Sinking slowly beneath the surface while maintaining unbroken eye contact, descending until only her reaching hand remains visible.
Calling out coordinates in a sailor's voice, luring a ship toward hidden rocks, her face beautiful and merciless in the fog.
